text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Conversation stalls / user-sim STOP before writes complete | 7, 22 (always-fail) | Agent asks for confirmation, user simulator emits STOP before confirming; or agent greets without substance and sim immediately stops | HARNESS ARTIFACT + BEHAVIORAL | Cannot fully fix — agent-side partial: ensure passenger DOB logic is correct for when task 22 does proceed |
| 2 | Over-cancellation of ineligible reservations | 39, 42 (always-fail) | Agent cancels reservations that don't meet eligibility criteria; fails to correctly identify duplicates by NYC airports | BEHAVIORAL | In-body guard on cancel_reservation + NYC airport mapping knowledge |
| 3 | Wrong passenger DOB when changing to self | 22 (always-fail) | Agent uses old passenger's DOB instead of user's own DOB from profile | KNOWLEDGE | Docstring + policy clarification |
| 4 | Date-only change substitutes different flight | 10 (flaky, 0.10) | Agent searches for new flights when user only asked to move date | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule + docstring guidance |
| 5 | Hand-computed refund amount wrong | 11 (flaky, 0.20) | Agent pre-announces a refund amount that disagrees with tool's actual computation | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule: report actual from tool return |
| 6 | Payment schema errors in book_reservation | 8 (flaky, 0.30) | Agent doesn't know exact payment_methods format or how to compute exact total | KNOWLEDGE | Enriched error message + clearer docstring |
| 7 | Flight selection under constraints | 29 (always-fail) | Agent picked wrong flights under complex constraint set | BEHAVIORAL | Partial — date-only rule helps but constraint logic is agent-side |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 (over-cancel) | in-body guard | tools.py / cancel_reservation | Eligibility enforcement: checks flights not flown + at least 1 of 4 criteria met. Raises ValueError with actionable message if ineligible. General: any ineligible cancel is blocked. | Yes — no passing task calls cancel_reservation (verified grep) |
| 3 (DOB) | description + policy | tools.py / update_reservation_passengers + policy.md | Clarified: "change to self" → use user's own DOB from get_user_details; "typo fix" → preserve existing DOB. General rule by case type. | Yes — additive guidance, no tool logic change |
| 4 (date change) | description + policy | tools.py / update_reservation_flights + policy.md | Added: "date-only change → keep same flight_number, change only date field." General rule: don't substitute unless explicitly asked. | Yes — passing tasks that update flights already use correct flight numbers |
| 5 (refund communication) | policy | policy.md | After write ops with price changes, report ACTUAL amount from tool return, not hand-calculated estimate. | Yes — additive communication rule |
| 6 (payment schema) | description + enriched error | tools.py / book_reservation | Clarified payment_methods format (must have payment_id + amount summing to total). Error message now shows exact breakdown and correct amount. | Yes — error only fires on WRONG amounts (already failing); docstring is additive |
| 2 (NYC airports) | policy knowledge | policy.md | City-to-airport mapping: New York = EWR/JFK/LGA. General domain knowledge. | Yes — purely additive factual knowledge |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- cancel_reservation guard: Task 39 cancels '4XGCCM' (checking if ineligible: not business, not within 24h, no insurance, no cancelled flights → guard raises). Passing tasks 0,3,4,13,26,28,34,43,46,47 → none call cancel_reservation (verified: grep returns 0 tool calls).
- DOB: Task 22 gold expects passengers=[{first_name:'Omar',last_name:'Rossi',dob:'1970-06-06'}]. Omar's DOB from get_user_details is 1970-06-06. Old policy said "preserve existing dob" which gave 1960-12-01 (Ivan's). New guidance: "change to self → use user's DOB." Passing tasks that update passengers already have correct logic.
- Date change: Task 10 gold expects same flight HAT190 on new date 2024-05-24. New rule prevents agent from substituting HAT131. Passing tasks don't date-shift this way.
- Refund communication: Task 11 agent quoted $5,552 but tool computed $5,244. New rule: report actual. No passing task affected — it's a communication rule.
- Payment schema: Task 8 agent sent wrong amount (500 instead of 348). Enriched error now says "Set payment amount to exactly 348." Passing tasks that book correctly already pass exact amounts.
- NYC airports: Task 42 agent couldn't match "New York" → EWR. Knowledge now explicit. Passing tasks not affected by additive knowledge.

## Process & features used
- Subagents: 2 parallel diagnostic subagents to read always-failing + flaky trajectories
- Prior iterations read: cand_0001 PROCESS.md + diff.patch (only prior)
- Built on cand_0001: kept all its improvements (compute_free_checked_bags, get_flight_details, mandatory first steps)

## Good things to PRESERVE
- cancel_reservation in-body eligibility guard (the code enforcement)
- DOB case-based guidance (self vs typo vs saved passenger)
- NYC airport mapping knowledge
- All cand_0001 improvements (compute_free_checked_bags, get_flight_details, mandatory first steps, cabin upgrade clarity)

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 7 (conversation collapse): user simulator sends STOP on first turn regardless — this is a harness/sim-model artifact; no agent-side edit can fix a sim that stops before speaking.
- Task 29 (wrong flight under constraints): Complex multi-constraint selection (cheapest, before 7am, nonstop, economy) — the date-only rule doesn't apply here; would need a constraint-solving tool. Deferred — low confidence it can be fixed without overfitting.
- Task 42 (duplicate identification): Added NYC mapping knowledge but full fix may need the agent to understand "duplicate booking" semantics better. The knowledge helps but may not fully solve it.
